ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the employee */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the employee */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the employee (F for female, M for male) */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "salary" INTEGER /* Annual salary of the employee */,
  "commission" INTEGER /* Commission earned by the employee */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the employee belongs to */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the employee */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information */,
  "hiring_status" TEXT /* Current hiring status (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* ID of the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Full name of the manager */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the manager oversees */,
  "total_direct_reports" INTEGER /* Total number of direct reports under the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the manager */,
  "address" TEXT /* Home address of the manager */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information */,
  "hiring_status" TEXT /* Current hiring status (e.g., active, terminated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_managers_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager overseeing the department */,
  "budget" INTEGER /* Budget allocated to the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department's responsibilities */,
  "headcount" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department most commonly associated with the job title */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Typical salary range for the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_job_titles_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance review */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being reviewed */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the performance review was conducted */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reviewer (usually a manager) */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given in the performance review (1-5) */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the reviewer */,
  "goals_met" INTEGER /* Number of goals met by the employee */,
  "improvement_areas" TEXT /* Areas where the employee needs improvement */,
  "next_review_date" TEXT /* Date of the next performance review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reviews_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the location */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
